mission.title: Install and Configure KubeRay
mission.description: KubeRay is an open-source Kubernetes operator designed to simplify the deployment and management of Ray applications on Kubernetes. It provides custom resource definitions for managing Ray clusters, jobs, and services, making it easier to run distributed workloads. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes and potential complexity in managing Ray applications.

steps.title: Add KubeRay Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeRay Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeRay using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kuberay https://ray-project.github.io/kuberay

helm repo update

```

> This command sets up the KubeRay Helm repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeRay Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the KubeRay operator in the 'kuberay-system' namespace, which is necessary for managing Ray resources.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kuberay-system

helm install kuberay kuberay/kuberay --namespace kuberay-system --version 1.5.1

```

> Creates a namespace for KubeRay resources.
> Installs KubeRay operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify KubeRay Installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the KubeRay operator is running correctly by listing the pods in the 'kuberay-system' namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay-system

```

> You should see the KubeRay operator pod running.

steps.title: Create a RayCluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a RayCluster resource, which defines the configuration for your Ray cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kuberay.io/v1alpha1
kind: RayCluster
metadata:
  name: example-raycluster
spec:
  head:
    replicas: 1
  worker:
    replicas: 2
EOF

```

> This YAML defines a RayCluster with 1 head and 2 worker nodes.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kuberay.io/v1alpha1
kind: RayCluster
metadata:
  name: example-raycluster
spec:
  head:
    replicas: 1
  worker:
    replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify RayCluster Status
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the RayCluster to ensure it is running as expected.

```bash

kubectl get raycluster example-raycluster

```

> You should see the status of the RayCluster and its components.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeRay Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the KubeRay operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all RayClusters and their data.

```bash

helm uninstall kuberay --namespace kuberay-system

kubectl delete namespace kuberay-system

```

> Uninstalls the KubeRay operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeRay Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step upgrades the KubeRay operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get raycluster -o yaml > raycluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kuberay kuberay/kuberay --namespace kuberay-system --version 1.5.1

kubectl get pods -n kuberay-system

```

> Backup existing RayCluster configurations.
> Upgrades the KubeRay operator.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating RayCluster: Failed to create head pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if there are insufficient resources in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and ensure there are enough CPU and memory available.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'RayCluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the RayCluster components are not fully initialized.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl describe raycluster example-raycluster' to check for events and issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Ray head'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the head pod is not running or is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the head pod with 'kubectl get pods -n kuberay-system'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Job failed with exit code 1'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that a Ray job has failed due to an error in the job specification.

**Fix:**
Review the job logs with 'kubectl logs <job-pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Ray features, improved stability and performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get raycluster example-raycluster' showing the cluster status as 'Running'.

spec:

    replicas: 1
  worker:
    replicas: 2
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
